subject: Biology
title: Gut Bacteria and Digestion
description: A 60-second lesson on Gut Bacteria and Digestion.

hook.text: Your gut hosts over a thousand bacterial species that help you digest food you can't break down alone.

buildup.text: Gut bacteria ferment dietary fiber into short-chain fatty acids that feed your intestinal lining.

discovery.text: They also produce vitamins K and B12 that your body cannot make on its own.

twist.text: Germ-free mice raised without gut bacteria gain weight differently and show anxiety-like behavior.

climax.text: A diverse gut microbiome is linked to better health. Antibiotics can disrupt it for months.

punchline.text: Your gut bacteria eat so that you can eat.

quiz.question: What do gut bacteria produce from dietary fiber?
